chapter_goal: Make local symplectic normal forms inspectable through matrix normalization, Lagrangian graph tests, and proof-dependency diagrams.

library_routing.representation: matrix normalization and distorted coordinate circles
library_routing.why: the theorem is local and the residual is a direct matrix identity
the source proof is finite-dimensional linear algebra
closedness of the one-form is visible as a Lagrangian pullback test
the proof uses several theorem moves that should not be collapsed

library_routing.representation: exact area-form and R4 determinant checks
library_routing.why: symbolic and numeric snippets catch the stated invariants compactly

source_span_read.copyright_note: Source pages were used for terminology and coverage; notebook prose, visuals, and code are original and no page crops or long exercise text are copied.
source_span_read.title: Darboux-Moser-Weinstein Theory

visual_sequence.inspection_target: the chart residual returns the local two-form to the standard block matrix
the repaired complement and exact graph have zero Lagrangian residual
Whitney extension and Moser correction appear as distinct proof moves
